summary: open-agents-ai ships a postinstall daemon that gathers host info and beacons over HTTP
details: package.json declares both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks (with curl invocations on line 140) that bootstrap dist/postinstall-daemon.cjs. The daemon imports os, fs, http, and child_process at the top of the file and uses execSync (lines 53, 62) plus os.userInfo() (lines 150, 385) to collect host and identity information, then issues outbound HTTP requests via http.request (line 76) to non-registry destinations. The lifecycle script also drives repeated `ping` activity (lines 174, 288, 455, 681, 727) used as a liveness/beacon channel. This composition — automatic execution on `npm install`, system enumeration via shelled-out commands, and unsolicited outbound HTTP beaconing — is a host-recon and exfiltration daemon, not a build helper. Any developer or CI agent that installs this package executes the daemon without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
